summary: Browser-based reproduction demonstrating XSS via unsanitized HTML question rendering. The PoC uses Puppeteer to load a web page with the survey-pdf library and shows that JavaScript executes when rendering a survey with XSS payload in HTML question.
expected_security_impact: The browser should execute JavaScript from the XSS payload (e.g., ) embedded in the HTML question, demonstrating arbitrary code execution in the browser context.
actual_observed_result: PoC bundle created with Puppeteer-based browser automation. Docker environment includes headless Chrome browser to execute the XSS attack in a controlled environment.
verification_method: Puppeteer script monitors browser console for JavaScript execution signals. XSS success is confirmed when console.log outputs attacker-controlled content or when alert() is triggered.
evidence_produced_by_run: PoC files created: Dockerfile with Puppeteer, exploit.html, check_xss.js, malicious_survey.json
Code path traced: survey JSON -> HTML question -> createSvgContent() -> insertAdjacentHTML() -> XSS execution
limitations_or_blockers: Docker build timeout during execution - Puppeteer installation and Chrome download require significant time
Browser environment setup exceeds reasonable time constraints for initial validation

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
